description: A polished 'self-deport' promotional graphic built around a warm, emotionally positive stock photograph: two people embracing in a tight hug, apparently in an airport terminal (blurred travelers and concourse lighting in the background). One person wears a denim jacket and a small backpack and has a face mask pulled down; the other wears a light-colored sweater. The photo evokes a happy reunion. Overlaid white text in the lower-left reads 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday —' with 'MAY' emphasized, followed by a large headline 'the LEGAL way' where the word 'LEGAL' is set in bold green to contrast with the white 'the' and 'way'. Across the bottom is a dark blue banner bearing the round DHS/ICE seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left, with a vertical divider and the call to action 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right. The propaganda technique pairs an upbeat, family-reunion image with the threat-softening promise of possible legal re-entry to encourage voluntary departure ('self-deportation'). Target audience: undocumented immigrants, urging them to leave the country before being arrested.
notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday — the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
